preview_text:
The model favors Turkey at 50%, a narrow margin over Paraguay's 20% win probability. The gap reflects a modest edge in expected strength, with the underdog offering roughly one-quarter of the favorite's winning chance.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 30%, narrowing the spread between full-time results. Turkey-Paraguay is forecast as a competitive match where a single goal or stalemate remain realistic scenarios alongside a Turkey victory. The distribution suggests meaningful uncertainty rather than a dominant performance.

Model and sportsbook estimates diverge on the tails. The model assigns Turkey 7 percentage points more win probability than the market consensus, while the market prices Paraguay 7 points higher. The draw sits nearly aligned across sources. The dashboard offers no view on which framing is correct; this divergence flags a genuine interpretive disagreement about the match likelihood.
